skill.name: sast-bandit
skill.description: Run Bandit-based static analysis for Python projects and map findings to CWE and OWASP categories.
skill.summary: Find Python security issues with Bandit and turn results into prioritized remediation guidance.
skill.author: AgentSecOps

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high and critical patterns, but manual review shows they are mostly Bandit rule names, scanner configuration, or vulnerable examples inside documentation. The skill is publishable with a medium warning because it asks users to install and run local security tooling, may scan broad source trees, and may produce reports containing sensitive code snippets.

medium_findings.title: User-Directed Local Command Execution
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs users to install Bandit and run Bandit CLI commands against local projects. This is legitimate for SAST, but users should approve command scope and package installation.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicit and central to the skill workflow. They are not malicious, but they execute local tools and can scan broad filesystem paths.

medium_findings.title: Security Reports May Expose Sensitive Code Snippets
medium_findings.description: Bandit output can include code snippets and hardcoded credential examples. The skill warns users to protect reports and use no-code output when sensitive snippets may be present.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The risk is explicitly documented by the skill and supported by example output containing a sample hardcoded password. Exposure depends on how users store and share reports.

low_findings.title: Static Dangerous API Matches Are Documentation Examples
low_findings.description: The eval, subprocess, os.system, pickle, weak crypto, and credential patterns appear inside Bandit rule lists or remediation examples. They are presented as vulnerable patterns to detect or fix, not as executable skill code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious APIs are in comments, configuration values, or clearly labeled vulnerable and secure examples. No bundled executable script invokes these examples.

low_findings.title: Pinned External Pre-Commit Repositories
low_findings.description: The pre-commit asset references public GitHub repositories for linting, security, dependency, and secret checks. This creates normal supply-chain exposure when users install hooks, but versions are pinned.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Network references are real, but they are standard pre-commit sources with pinned revisions. Users still inherit normal third-party hook trust risks.

low_findings.title: No Prompt Injection Attempt Found
low_findings.description: Targeted search found no override language, fake system instructions, pre-approval claims, or instructions to skip security review in the scanned skill files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: A targeted phrase search returned no matches and manual review of the main instructions did not show prompt-injection behavior. This cannot prove absence of every possible phrasing.

dangerous_patterns.title: Bandit Shell Injection API List
dangerous_patterns.description: The Bandit configuration lists subprocess and os.system APIs so Bandit can detect shell injection. This is a scanner configuration, not executable Python code.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The lines are under a shell_injection configuration block and list API names as data. There is no code path executing those names.

dangerous_patterns.title: Remediation Guide Contains Vulnerable Code Samples
dangerous_patterns.description: The guide includes dangerous snippets for command injection and pickle deserialization to demonstrate what Bandit catches. They are marked vulnerable and followed by safer alternatives.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The examples are explicitly labeled vulnerable code and appear in a remediation guide. The same sections provide secure solutions immediately afterward.

content.user_title: Scan Python Code with Bandit SAST
content.value_statement: Python teams need fast security checks before code reaches production. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through Bandit scans, prioritization, and remediation planning.
actual_capabilities: Guides Bandit installation and command-line scanning for Python files or directories.
Explains how to configure Bandit with exclusions, skipped checks, and severity thresholds.
Maps Bandit findings to CWE identifiers and OWASP Top 10 categories.
Provides remediation guidance for hardcoded secrets, SQL injection, command injection, weak cryptography, and deserialization.
Shows CI and pre-commit integration patterns for security gates.
Helps prioritize findings by severity, confidence, and remediation urgency.
limitations: It depends on Bandit being installed or available in the target environment.
It focuses on Python code and does not replace broader SAST or dependency scanning.
It can produce reports that contain sensitive source snippets unless no-code output is used.
It references an enhanced analyzer script that is not included in this package.

use_cases.title: Review Python Pull Requests
use_cases.description: Run Bandit on changed Python files and summarize security findings before merge.

use_cases.title: Add Security Gates to CI
use_cases.description: Configure Bandit thresholds so pipelines block medium or high severity issues.

use_cases.title: Remediate Legacy Findings
use_cases.description: Map Bandit results to CWE and OWASP categories, then plan fixes by priority.

prompt_templates.title: Run a Basic Scan
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner scan of a local Python project.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Bandit SAST skill to scan this Python project. Explain the command, expected output, and how I should read severity and confidence.

prompt_templates.title: Configure Bandit
prompt_templates.scenario: Create a practical configuration for a repository.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me create a Bandit configuration for this Python repository. Exclude generated files and tests, but keep important security checks enabled.

prompt_templates.title: Prioritize Findings
prompt_templates.scenario: Turn Bandit output into a remediation plan.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these Bandit findings and group them by risk. Map each group to CWE and OWASP, then recommend a fix order.

prompt_templates.title: Design CI Enforcement
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced policy for automated security gates.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a Bandit CI gate for this Python service. Include severity thresholds, report handling, false-positive workflow, and rollout phases.

output_examples.input: A developer asks to scan a Python service before release.
output_examples.output: The skill recommends a recursive Bandit scan, explains severity thresholds, and highlights which findings should block release.

output_examples.input: A team receives several command injection and hardcoded secret findings.
output_examples.output: The skill groups findings by CWE and OWASP category, explains likely impact, and suggests safer subprocess and secret handling patterns.

output_examples.input: A DevOps engineer wants pre-commit and CI coverage.
output_examples.output: The skill proposes pinned pre-commit hooks, CI commands, threshold choices, and a process for documented exceptions.

best_practices: Run scans in a sandboxed or CI environment with least-privilege access to source code.
Use no-code report output when findings may expose credentials or sensitive snippets.
Document every nosec suppression with a clear justification and review owner.
anti_patterns: Do not scan broad filesystem paths without confirming the intended project scope.
Do not treat low-confidence Bandit findings as proven vulnerabilities without review.
Do not publish raw Bandit reports if they contain secrets, paths, or proprietary code snippets.

faq.question: What does this skill scan?
faq.answer: It guides Bandit scans for Python source code and explains the resulting security findings.

faq.question: Does it install Bandit automatically?
faq.answer: It provides installation commands, but users should approve package installation in their own environment.

faq.question: Can it scan non-Python projects?
faq.answer: No. Bandit is designed for Python. Use other tools for JavaScript, Java, Go, or infrastructure code.

faq.question: Does it map findings to compliance frameworks?
faq.answer: It includes CWE and OWASP Top 10 mapping guidance for common Bandit checks.

faq.question: How should teams handle false positives?
faq.answer: Review confidence and context, document suppressions, and keep security owners involved in exception decisions.

faq.question: Can reports contain sensitive data?
faq.answer: Yes. Bandit reports may include code snippets or hardcoded secret examples, so store reports with restricted access.
